info.title: EDON Governance API
info.description: Real-time governance for AI agents. Every action evaluated, every decision auditable.

post.description:
Evaluate an agent action through the governance engine.

This is the primary endpoint for agent action evaluation. It:
1. Validates the request
2. Parses action_type into tool and operation
3. Loads the relevant intent contract (from context or active policy)
4. Evaluates the action through the policy engine
5. Logs the decision to the audit trail
6. Returns the governance decision with latency metrics

Args:
    request: FastAPI request object (for tenant context)
    req: V1ActionRequest containing action details

Returns:
    V1ActionResponse with governance decision and metadata

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 400: Invalid request format or action_type
        - 500: Internal error during evaluation

get.description: List all intent contracts for this tenant, newest first.

delete.description: Delete an intent contract by ID.

get.description: Look up the governance decision for a specific action ID.

Verify cryptographic integrity of audit trail hash chain.

Returns verification result including whether chain is valid,
how many entries were checked, and where breakage occurred if any.

Export audit trail in JSON, CSV, or Parquet format.

Implements Spec 4.8: paginated export with optional chain verification proof.
Get human review queue for actions requiring human decision.

post.description: Enqueue an action for human review (called internally by governor).

Submit human decision for a queued review.

Body: {"decision": "approve"|"block", "reason": "optional reason"}

get.description:
List all policy rules for the current tenant.

Rules are ordered by priority (highest first).

post.description:
Create a new custom policy rule for the current tenant.

Rules are evaluated before the standard governance policy.
The first matching rule (by priority, highest first) wins.

**Conditions** (all are optional; omitting means "match any"):
- `condition_tool`: e.g. `robot`, `email`, `shell`
- `condition_op`: e.g. `move`, `send`, `exec`
- `condition_risk_level`: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`
- `condition_tags`: list of tags that must ALL be present

**Actions**: `ALLOW`, `BLOCK`, `ESCALATE`

put.description:
Update a policy rule.

If the rule is protected (seeded by Clinical Safety Mode), the caller must
have **admin** role and supply a non-empty `justification` field.
The justification is appended to the policy-change audit log.

delete.description:
Delete a policy rule.

If the rule is protected (seeded by Clinical Safety Mode), the caller must
have **admin** role and supply a non-empty `justification` in the request body.

post.description:
Disable a policy rule without deleting it.

If the rule is protected (seeded by Clinical Safety Mode), the caller must
have **admin** role and supply a non-empty `justification` in the request body.

get.summary: Gateway Ed25519 public key for decision verification
get.description:
Return the gateway's Ed25519 public key so customers can verify decision signatures.

Each Decision returned by POST /v1/action includes ``meta.sig`` (base64url Ed25519
signature) and ``meta.kid`` (first 16 hex chars of the public key). Verify offline::

    from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PublicKey
    import base64, json
    pub = Ed25519PublicKey.from_public_bytes(bytes.fromhex(pubkey_hex))
    pub.verify(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(sig + "=="), canonical_payload)
Return identity info for the current API key.

post.description:
Rotate an API key with zero-downtime overlap.

Creates a new API key and marks the current key as **rotating** (still valid
for `overlap_hours`). During the overlap window both keys authenticate
successfully so you can deploy the new key without any auth failures.

After `overlap_hours` the old key automatically stops being accepted.

Returns the **new plaintext key** — store it securely, it is shown only once.

get.description:
List API keys for the current tenant.

Never returns the plaintext key or its hash — only metadata.

post.description:
Create a new API key for the current tenant.

Returns the **plaintext key** once — it is never stored and cannot be retrieved again.
Store it securely immediately.

delete.description:
Revoke/delete an API key for the current tenant.

Verifies tenant ownership before revoking. Revoked keys are immediately rejected.

post.description:
Register or update a full agent profile for the authenticated tenant.

Uses db.register_agent_full (upsert) — idempotent for the same agent_id.

get.description: List all agents registered for the authenticated tenant.
Return full agent profile with 7-day trend, top tools, and top block reasons.

Return 30-day time-series stats for a specific agent.

Includes daily action counts by verdict, verdict breakdown totals,
risk level breakdown, top block reasons, 7-day trend, and 24h
behavioral CAV history (hourly buckets).

Return the tenant's current shadow mode setting.

In shadow mode, all decisions are evaluated and logged but NEVER blocked —
every verdict is overridden to 'allowed'. Use this for trials and pilots.

post.description:
Enable or disable shadow mode for the tenant.

When enabled, EDON evaluates every action and logs the true verdict, but
always responds with 'allowed' — nothing is blocked. Ideal for hospital
trials where you want to observe governance decisions without disruption.

get.description: List all configured webhooks for this tenant.

post.description: Register a webhook endpoint to receive governance events.
Send a test payload to the webhook URL.

Record an execution outcome for a previously evaluated action.

Called by agents after a tool runs. Links the governance decision
(action_id) to the real-world outcome (success / failure / timeout).

Returns the assigned result_id for the caller's records.

get.description: Look up the recorded execution outcome for a specific action_id.

Return the current kill switch state for this tenant.

active=true means ALL agent actions are being blocked right now.

post.description:
Activate the kill switch — immediately halt ALL AI agent actions.

Every /v1/action call for this tenant will return BLOCK until the kill
switch is deactivated. The real governance verdict is still computed and
audit-logged, but the response is always BLOCK.

Use this when:
- A security incident is suspected
- An agent is behaving unexpectedly
- You need an immediate pause before a policy update

This action is audit-logged with timestamp and reason.

delete.description:
Deactivate the kill switch — resume normal governance.

Normal policy evaluation resumes immediately. This action is audit-logged.

action_id.title: Action Id
action_id.description: The action_id returned by the /v1/action response for this execution

action_type.title: Action Type
action_type.description: The action type that was executed (e.g. 'email.send')

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.description: Execution outcome: success | failure | partial | timeout

latency_ms.title: Latency Ms
latency_ms.description: Tool execution time in milliseconds (not governance latency)

executed_at.title: Executed At
executed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when tool execution completed

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if outcome is failure or timeout (max 1000 chars, enforced server-side)

result_summary.title: Result Summary
result_summary.description: Optional sanitized one-line description of what happened. Do NOT include PII, credentials, or full response bodies. Max 500 chars, enforced server-side.

ActionResultRequest.title: ActionResultRequest
ActionResultRequest.description:
Posted by an agent after executing a tool action.

The action_id field must match the action_id returned by the preceding
/v1/action call. This closes the loop so EDON can correlate governance
decisions with real execution outcomes.

result_id.title: Result Id
result_id.description: Unique ID assigned to this result record

ActionResultResponse.title: ActionResultResponse
ActionResultResponse.description: Confirmation returned after recording an action result.

vendor_id.title: Vendor Id
vendor_id.description: Vendor that owns/operates this agent, e.g. 'vendor-a-surgical'

role.title: Role
role.description: RBAC role: admin, operator, user, read_only

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why you are halting all AI agents for this tenant

activated_by.title: Activated By
activated_by.description: Identity of the person activating the kill switch

justification.title: Justification
justification.description: Required when deleting a protected (clinical safety) rule. Admin role only.

justification.title: Justification
justification.description: Required when disabling a protected (clinical safety) rule. Admin role only.

justification.title: Justification
justification.description: Required when mutating a protected (clinical safety) rule. Admin role only.

overlap_hours.title: Overlap Hours
overlap_hours.description: How long (hours) the old key stays valid after rotation. Default 24h, max 168h (7 days).

name.title: Name
name.description: Optional name for the new key

agent_id.title: Agent Id
agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent making the request

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when action was requested

device_id.title: Device Id
device_id.description: Physical device ID being commanded (e.g. 'sr-001'). When present, EDON enforces agent-device binding and mutex lock.

V1ActionRequest.title: V1ActionRequest
V1ActionRequest.description:
Request schema for /v1/action endpoint.

This is the primary request format for agents to submit actions for evaluation.
All fields are required for proper governance and audit trail.

action_id.title: Action Id
action_id.description: Unique identifier for this action (for audit trail)

decision.title: Decision
decision.description: Governance decision: ALLOW, BLOCK, DEGRADE, HUMAN_REQUIRED, ESCALATE, or PAUSE

decision_reason.title: Decision Reason
decision_reason.example: Action approved: within scope, constraints satisfied, risk acceptable

policy_version.title: Policy Version
policy_version.description: Version of the policy used for evaluation

processing_latency_ms.title: Processing Latency Ms
processing_latency_ms.description: Time taken to evaluate the action in milliseconds

reason_code.title: Reason Code
reason_code.description: Machine-readable reason code for the decision

safe_alternative.title: Safe Alternative
safe_alternative.description: Alternative action if decision is DEGRADE

escalation_question.title: Escalation Question
escalation_question.description: Question to ask user if decision is ESCALATE or HUMAN_REQUIRED

escalation_options.title: Escalation Options
escalation_options.description: Options for user to choose from if decision is ESCALATE

predicted_oob_risk.title: Predicted Oob Risk
predicted_oob_risk.description: Predicted out-of-bounds risk score (0.0-1.0) when available

predicted_oob_reasons.title: Predicted Oob Reasons
predicted_oob_reasons.description: Human-readable reasons that contributed to predicted_oob_risk
example: tool/op not seen in agent 30-day baseline

predicted_oob_breakdown.title: Predicted Oob Breakdown
predicted_oob_breakdown.description: Signal-level weighted contributions used to compute predicted_oob_risk

shadow_mode.title: Shadow Mode
shadow_mode.description: True when the tenant has shadow mode enabled — decision was evaluated but verdict was overridden to ALLOW. The real verdict is in the audit log.

V1ActionResponse.title: V1ActionResponse
V1ActionResponse.description:
Response schema for /v1/action endpoint.

Returns the governance decision and metadata about the evaluation.

example.decision_reason: Action approved: within scope, constraints satisfied, risk acceptable

tags.name: governance
tags.description: Evaluate agent actions and verify decisions

tags.name: audit
tags.description: Query and export the audit log
